feedback.text: I think one of the major outstanding issues of the web today is the systematic malicious compliance of every stakeholder with the RGPD's consent gathering articles. Many analytics providers offer tools that clearly ignore the "Opting out should be as easy as opting in law". Lots of websites keep using these analytics tools, which means they keep up the same "collect all data" by default practices and just slap a pop-up on it. The truth is that data collection is never in the end-user's interest, and the GDPR's rules about collections amount to forcing companies to find the most dishonest way to ask users "Can you please let us spy on you forever because it's more convenient for us?". Even this site, ec.europa.eu, has a pointless cookie banner. Future laws should just cut the middleman and forbid a broad range of analytics. Google has already done serious work on analytics technologies which do not require individualized tracking. The vast majority of information that webmasters get from analytics can be derived from anonymized, binned data that does not require consent. It's time to get rid of cookie pop-ups and finish the job.
